# Dashfy

> Dashboards for developers. Define dashboards as code. Connect APIs. Render real-time interfaces.

## Site

- [Home](https://dashfy.dev/): Product overview, features, config examples, and use cases
- [Extensions](https://dashfy.dev/extensions): Official integrations and connectors
- [Docs](https://dashfy.dev/docs): Installation, configuration, CLI, and extension docs
- [Brand](https://dashfy.dev/brand): Logos, icons, screenshots, and brand guidelines

## Get started

- [Introduction](https://dashfy.dev/docs): Dashfy is an open-source framework for building dashboards using declarative configuration.
- [Installation](https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation): Scaffold a new Dashfy project with the CLI, or add Dashfy to an existing app.
- [Quick start](https://dashfy.dev/docs/quick-start): Create a Dashfy server, load a dashboard configuration, and render widgets in React.

## Guides

- [Configuration](https://dashfy.dev/docs/configuration): Define Dashfy dashboards declaratively using TypeScript, JSON, or YAML.
- [How it works](https://dashfy.dev/docs/how-it-works): Dashfy uses a server–client architecture to load, compose, and render dashboards in real time.
- [Deployment](https://dashfy.dev/docs/deployment): Deploy a Dashfy app to production. Railway is the recommended one-click host.
- [Extensions](https://dashfy.dev/docs/extensions): Extensions add widgets and data sources to Dashfy. Add, remove, and audit them with the CLI.
- [Themes](https://dashfy.dev/docs/themes): Built-in themes, light/dark mode, and custom theming with @getdashfy/themes.

## Reference

- [CLI](https://dashfy.dev/docs/cli): The dashfy CLI scaffolds dashboards, manages extensions, and audits your project.
- [Registry](https://dashfy.dev/docs/registry): How Dashfy resolves extensions from registries, and how to author and host your own.
- [Skills](https://dashfy.dev/docs/skills): Give your AI agent project-aware knowledge of Dashfy — scaffolding, extensions, widgets, and dashboard layout.
- [MCP server](https://dashfy.dev/docs/mcp): Run a Model Context Protocol server so AI assistants can discover and install Dashfy extensions.
- [Cursor plugin](https://dashfy.dev/docs/cursor-plugin): Bundle the Dashfy agent skill, project rules, and MCP server as a single Cursor plugin.

## Installation guides

Framework-specific setup guides:

- [Astro](https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation/astro): Scaffold a Dashfy dashboard with Astro, or add Dashfy to an existing Astro app.
- [Manual](https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation/manual): Add Dashfy to any React app by hand, without the CLI.
- [Next.js](https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation/next): Scaffold a Dashfy dashboard with Next.js, or add Dashfy to an existing Next.js app.
- [React Router](https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation/react-router): Scaffold a Dashfy dashboard with React Router, or add Dashfy to an existing React Router app.
- [TanStack Start](https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation/tanstack): Scaffold a Dashfy dashboard with TanStack Start, or add Dashfy to an existing TanStack Start app.
- [Vite](https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation/vite): Scaffold a Dashfy dashboard with Vite, or add Dashfy to an existing Vite app.

## CLI commands

Scaffold a new project:

- pnpm: `pnpm dlx dashfy@latest init`
- npm: `npx dashfy@latest init`
- yarn: `yarn dlx dashfy@latest init`
- bun: `bunx dashfy@latest init`

Add the Dashfy skill to an AI agent:

- pnpm: `pnpm dlx dashfy@latest add skill`
- npm: `npx dashfy@latest add skill`
- yarn: `yarn dlx dashfy@latest add skill`
- bun: `bunx dashfy@latest add skill`

Add an extension (GitHub example):

- pnpm: `pnpm dlx dashfy@latest add @getdashfy/github`
- npm: `npx dashfy@latest add @getdashfy/github`
- yarn: `yarn dlx dashfy@latest add @getdashfy/github`
- bun: `bunx dashfy@latest add @getdashfy/github`

## Extensions

Available now (5): GitHub, JSON / REST, Market Live, NBA, System Monitoring. Full catalog: https://dashfy.dev/extensions

Coming soon (28): AWS, Claude AI, Cloudflare, Cursor, Datadog, Discord, Docker, Firebase, Gemini, GitLab, Google Sheets, Grafana, Jira, Kubernetes, MongoDB, Notion, Open Claw, OpenAI, PostgreSQL, Prometheus, Redis, Resend, Sentry, Slack, Stripe, Supabase, Telegram, Vercel

## Raw documentation (markdown)

Any docs page is also available as plain markdown for AI tools — append `.md` to the docs URL:

- [Introduction](https://dashfy.dev/docs.md)
- [Installation](https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation.md)
- [Configuration](https://dashfy.dev/docs/configuration.md)
- [CLI](https://dashfy.dev/docs/cli.md)

Pattern: `https://dashfy.dev/docs/<slug>.md` (example: `https://dashfy.dev/docs/installation/next.md`).

## External resources

- [Live demo](https://demo.dashfy.dev)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/dashfy)
- [Roadmap](https://dashfy.dev/roadmap)
- [Sponsor](https://dashfy.dev/sponsor)
- [Discord](https://dashfy.dev/discord)
- [X (Twitter)](https://x.com/dashfydev)

## Contact

- Email: contact@dashfy.dev

## Notes for AI agents

- Brand name is "Dashfy" (capital D, single word). Do not alter brand assets from /brand.
- Prefer the raw markdown docs (`.md` URLs) over scraping HTML when possible.
- Dashfy extensions use the `@getdashfy/<name>` namespace (e.g. `@getdashfy/github`).
- Do not invent API references or config options not shown on the site, docs, or GitHub repo.
